title: Molten One-Pot Mediterranean Salmon Tagliere
description: A spicy, satvik-friendly one-pot dish featuring salmon, tandoor, and Mediterranean flavors, all cooked to perfection in a tagliere.

ingredients.name: Salmon fillets (6 oz)
ingredients.notes: Fresh, wild-caught salmon, preferably sustainably sourced.

ingredients.name: Tandoori masala
ingredients.notes: Homemade or store-bought, adjust to taste.

ingredients.name: Red pepper flakes
ingredients.notes: Adjust to desired level of spiciness.

instructions: In a large tagliere, combine salmon fillets, tandoori masala, lemon juice, garlic, ginger, olive oil, and red pepper flakes. Toss to coat evenly.
Add feta cheese, salt, and black pepper to the tagliere, toss to combine.
Transfer the tagliere to the preheated oven and bake for 12-15 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through and the cheese is melted.

While the salmon is baking, heat the tomato paste and cherry tomatoes in a small skillet over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes have released their juices and the paste has thickened.
To assemble the dish, place a cooked salmon fillet on each plate, top with a spoonful of the tomato mixture, and garnish with chopped fresh parsley and basil.
